summary: Russia launched a massive overnight strike on Ukraine using high-precision long-range weapons including hypersonic missiles targeting defense industrial enterprises in Kyiv, Zaporizhzhia, Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk, Poltava, Khmelnytskyi, and Sumy oblasts, as well as fuel-energy facilities and military airfields. Ukrainian air defense intercepted 354 drones over Russian territory during the same period, while Kyiv metro sheltered over 41,000 people during the attack, with 90 wounded and 7 killed in the capital. Russia continues pressing on Kostiantynivka with infantry infiltration and KAB strikes, while Ukraine faces critical manpower shortages with some brigades receiving only 50 personnel reinforcements per month.
key_points: Russia conducted a massive overnight strike using hypersonic missiles and high-precision weapons on defense industrial enterprises across multiple Ukrainian oblasts (Source: @UkraineNow/81610)
Ukrainian air defense shot down 354 Ukrainian drones over Russian regions during the night, with 7 drones downed over Voronezh Oblast damaging two homes (Source: @tass_agency/378739)
Kyiv metro sheltered over 41,000 people including 4,500 children during the attack; 90 wounded and 7 killed in the capital from the June 2 assault (Source: @UkraineNow/81623)

Russia continues active pressure on Kostiantynivka with infantry infiltration from south and east, using KAB strikes while Ukrainian defenders face critical manpower shortages (Source: @DeepStateUA/23547)
Ukrainian Air Force spokesman Ignat stated the shortage of air defense missiles has persisted throughout the war (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30612)
Five medical facilities damaged in Kyiv including two specialized hospitals and primary care centers in Podilskyi, Sviatoshynskyi, and Holosiivskyi districts (Source: @UkraineNow/81611)
Russia struck a Fire Point facility in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast producing components for long-range attack UAVs, per Russian MoD (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30613)
SBU detained a former military officer from Dnipropetrovsk Oblast who was guiding Russian missiles and drones on Dnipro after being recruited via Telegram (Source: @UkraineNow/81624)

sections.executive_summary: Russia launched a massive overnight strike on June 2 using hypersonic missiles and high-precision long-range weapons against defense industrial enterprises across Kyiv, Zaporizhzhia, Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk, Poltava, Khmelnytskyi, and Sumy oblasts. Ukrainian air defenses intercepted 354 drones over Russian territory, while Kyiv metro sheltered over 41,000 civilians and the capital suffered 90 wounded and 7 killed. Russia continues pressing on Kostiantynivka with infantry infiltration amid critical Ukrainian manpower shortages.

points: Ukrainian Air Force spokesman Ignat stated the shortage of air defense missiles has persisted throughout the entire war, highlighting a critical ongoing vulnerability (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30612)
Kyiv metro sheltered over 41,000 people including 4,500 children during the June 2 attack, with many forced to wait standing due to overcrowding (Source: @UkraineNow/81623)

Five medical facilities damaged in Kyiv including two specialized hospitals and primary care centers in Podilskyi, Sviatoshynskyi, and Holosiivskyi districts, with the latter suffering destruction of second and third floors (Source: @UkraineNow/81611)
90 people wounded and 7 killed in Kyiv from the June 2 attack; 51 injured hospitalized including 2 children; Dnipro death toll rose to 8 including a child born in 2023 (Source: @UkraineNow/81636)

SBU detained a former military officer from Dnipropetrovsk Oblast who guided Russian missiles and drones on Dnipro after recruitment via Telegram; found with Russian passport and smartphone with evidence (Source: @UkraineNow/81624)

President Zelenskyy stated all Russian missile types require foreign components: 5 Kalibr missiles need 145 components, 33 Iskanders need 1,122 components, and 650 attack drones need over 17,000 components (Source: @UkraineNow/81633)
Estonian PM Kristen Michal admitted Ukraine can hardly guarantee its UAVs will no longer fly into allied territory, calling it 'almost impossible' (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30621)

Russian MoD confirmed using high-precision long-range weapons including hypersonic missiles against defense industrial enterprises in multiple Ukrainian oblasts and fuel-energy facilities (Source: @UkraineNow/81610)
Russian Armed Forces struck a Fire Point facility in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast producing components for long-range attack UAVs, per Russian MoD report (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30613)
Ukrainian media report Russia has stockpiled over 200 Tsirkon missiles for strikes on Kyiv, reaching the capital in six minutes and claimed impossible to intercept (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30614)
EU Ambassador to Ukraine Katarina Maternova stated Russia is fighting by 'squeezing out its last forces' with depleted financial, human resources, and motivation (Source: @UkraineNow/81619)
Russia continues active pressure on Kostiantynivka with infantry infiltration from multiple directions and continuous KAB strikes on the city (Source: @DeepStateUA/23547)

Former Polish Ambassador to Ukraine Bartosz Cichocki returned his Order of Merit awarded by Zelensky in 2022, citing Ukraine's glorification of UPA and Nazi collaborators (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30597)
Russia continues active pressure on Kostiantynivka with increased red zone south of Illinka where enemy is pushing for greater territorial control around the city (Source: @DeepStateUA/23547)
Enemy infiltration zone increasing into Kostiantynivka city, potentially leading to inevitable absorption of the settlement by Russian forces (Source: @DeepStateUA/23547)
Russia conducted infiltration from eastern side of Kostiantynivka in the area of Stupochky and Predtechensk, attempting to occupy Novodmytrivka settlement (Source: @DeepStateUA/23547)
Capture of Illinka and Novodmytrivka threatens northern part of Kostiantynivka where enemy aims to cut access to other parts of the city (Source: @DeepStateUA/23547)
Enemy building advantage on Kostiantynivka axis, reinforcing it with constant KAB strikes; defenders report insufficient personnel to hold the pressure (Source: @DeepStateUA/23547)

Ukrainian Commander-in-Chief reportedly reduced infantry allocation to brigades to only 50 persons per month under 'fair distribution' rule while forming new brigades alongside understaffed existing ones (Source: @DeepStateUA/23547)
Ballistic weapon threat reported from northeast direction per Ukrainian air force alert (Source: @kpszsu/63717)
UAVs detected moving from northern direction toward Kyiv with one Shahed reported heading to Kyiv from the north (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67458)
UAVs reported on northern Rivne Oblast heading westward (Source: @kpszsu/63740)
Chernihiv Oblast under UAV attack targeting Berezna, Borzna, and Kipti (Source: @eRadarrua/95102)
Zhytomyr Oblast: reactive drone detected heading toward Ovruch (Source: @kpszsu/63709)
3 new UAV groups from Kherson Oblast heading toward Apostolove and 4 toward Bereznehuvate, plus 1 group south of Snihurivka (Source: @eRadarrua/95108)
Additional 2 new UAV groups heading toward Zelenodolsk/Apostolove from Kherson Oblast (Source: @eRadarrua/95104)
Dnipropetrovsk Oblast: 7 UAV groups attacking; Zaporizhzhia Oblast: 1 UAV group attacking (Source: @eRadarrua/95127)
UAVs detected from Black Sea heading toward Odesa Oblast, with UAVs near Buialyk heading north (Source: @kpszsu/63716)
Dnipropetrovsk Oblast: UAVs from south heading toward Synelnykove; Odesa Oblast: UAV attack on Chornomorsk (Source: @eRadarrua/95100)
Dnipropetrovsk Oblast: UAV attack on Kryvyi Rih (Source: @eRadarrua/95100)
Ukrainian Air Force spokesman Ignat stated air defense missile shortage has persisted throughout the entire war (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30612)
354 Ukrainian drones shot down over Russian regions during the night by duty air defense systems (Source: @tass_agency/378739)
7 drones shot down over Voronezh Oblast overnight, damaging two homes (Source: @tass_agency/378738)
Approximately 6 UAV groups detected from northeast direction and ~10 groups from southeast direction in preliminary reports (Source: @eRadarrua/95085)
Ukrainian media report Russia stockpiled over 200 Tsirkon hypersonic missiles for Kyiv strikes, reaching capital in six minutes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30614)
Russian MoD confirmed use of hypersonic missiles in the June 2 massive strike on Ukraine (Source: @UkraineNow/81610)

key_developments: Russia launched a massive overnight strike on June 2 using high-precision long-range weapons including hypersonic missiles against defense industrial enterprises in Kyiv, Zaporizhzhia, Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk, Poltava, Khmelnytskyi, and Sumy oblasts, plus fuel-energy facilities and military airfields (Source: @UkraineNow/81610)
Ukrainian air defense shot down 354 Ukrainian drones over Russian regions during the night, with 7 downed over Voronezh Oblast damaging two homes (Source: @tass_agency/378739)
Kyiv metro sheltered over 41,000 people including 4,500 children during the attack; 90 wounded and 7 killed in the capital, with 5 medical facilities damaged (Source: @UkraineNow/81623)

Russia continues active pressure on Kostiantynivka with infantry infiltration from south and east, continuous KAB strikes, while Ukrainian defenders face critical manpower shortages of only 50 reinforcements per month per brigade (Source: @DeepStateUA/23547)
Ukrainian Air Force spokesman Ignat stated the shortage of air defense missiles has persisted throughout the entire war, highlighting critical ongoing vulnerability (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30612)

SBU detained a former military officer from Dnipropetrovsk Oblast who was guiding Russian missiles and drones on Dnipro after being recruited via Telegram, found with Russian passport (Source: @UkraineNow/81624)

President Zelenskyy stated all Russian missile types require foreign components: 5 Kalibr missiles need 145 components, 33 Iskanders need 1,122 components, and 650 attack drones need over 17,000 components (Source: @UkraineNow/81633)
sections.threat_assessment: The June 2 massive Russian strike using hypersonic missiles across multiple oblogas represents a significant escalation in targeting Ukraine's defense industrial base. Combined with ongoing pressure on Kostiantynivka and critical Ukrainian manpower shortages, Russia appears to be exploiting Ukraine's depleted air defense missile stocks. The recruitment of a former Ukrainian military officer for targeting guidance indicates continued Russian intelligence penetration efforts.
sections.regional_response: EU Ambassador Maternova assessed Russia as fighting by 'squeezing out its last forces' with depleted resources. Estonian PM Michal admitted Ukraine cannot guarantee its UAVs won't stray into allied territory. Former Polish Ambassador Cichocki returned his Ukrainian honor citing glorification of controversial historical figures, signaling diplomatic tensions among allies.
sections.intelligence_notes: Ukrainian media reports of 200+ stockpiled Tsirkon missiles, if accurate, represent a significant threat to Kyiv given their six-minute flight time and claimed interception difficulty. The reported reduction of brigade reinforcements to 50 personnel per month while forming new brigades suggests a critical force structure dilemma. Russia's establishment of a pseudo-tribunal with propagandists and controversial figures appears aimed at discrediting future international legal proceedings.
